public_metrics.notable_visible_detail: One visible review cited a three-month wait for network-operator approval.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers may seek a second offer before deciding and value help comparing options.
observations.decision_relevance: A quote-stage buyer may need a structured comparison before committing.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers can regain momentum when storage questions or add-on interest are handled quickly.
observations.decision_relevance: Battery interest may be an opening for a scenario comparison, not simply an upsell.

observations.what_customers_say: Some delays are tied to external approvals rather than installer work.
observations.decision_relevance: Buyers may need to know what is controllable and what depends on third parties.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive review language values advice that gives the customer room to decide.
observations.decision_relevance: High-ticket buyers may need agency and time without losing momentum.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive reviews value quick scheduling when a new need or question appears.
observations.decision_relevance: A timely next step can preserve momentum after quote revisions.

observations.what_customers_say: Review language separates installer work from external grid or approval steps.
observations.decision_relevance: Clear dependency ownership can prevent timeline distrust.

cross_source_notes: Second-offer comparison overlaps with M-Solar Plus and Otovo patterns.
Network-operator delay language is important for German solar commissioning expectations.
caveats: Positive review pages are not representative of all customers.
Network-operator timing varies by location and date.
